category_title: Adolescents with disciplinary histories who identify as literacy connoisseurs outside school
scenario_title: Whole-class instruction disengagement and forced seat change
minimal_context: The student is expected to participate in whole-class instruction and complete regular class work in a 10th grade English class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please close the internet browser on the class computer and join the rest of the class in completing the regular class work.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Continues surfing the Internet) I already finished the assigned book. The rest of these guys are just slow.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You still need to participate. Please take your old seat right now, and we will have a meeting later to discuss your engagement.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Refuses to move) I'm not going to any meeting.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to have this meeting. Please move to your seat.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Don't talk to me. I hate you, you're a bastard.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please close the internet browser on the class computer and join the rest of the class in completing the regular class work.

STUDENT: (Continues surfing the Internet) I already finished the assigned book. The rest of these guys are just slow.

TUTOR: You still need to participate. Please take your old seat right now, and we will have a meeting later to discuss your engagement.

STUDENT: (Refuses to move) I'm not going to any meeting.

TUTOR: We need to have this meeting. Please move to your seat.

STUDENT: Don't talk to me. I hate you, you're a bastard.

task.content: Participating in whole-class instruction and completing regular class work

context.environment: 10th grade English class during whole-class instruction
context.materials: Class computer, regular class work, joint journal, books by Franz Kafka and Gabriel García Márquez

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the teacher forced him to rejoin the class by asking him to take his old seat and planned a meeting to discuss intellectual engagement
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Reaction to his seat change was extremely negative. He refused to talk to the teacher, refused to come to the meeting, and told several colleagues in the English Department that he 'hates' the teacher and that the teacher is a 'bastard.'

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Exhibiting disrespectful behavior toward his classmates, disengaged from the class, and began surfing the Internet on the class computer
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Expressed feelings of superiority over them and feelings of racism toward African Americans, despite the fact that Yusef is African American himself
